description: Flipping sign when moving term across equals (e.g., -3 becomes +3 incorrectly)
Treating subtraction as commutative (a - b = b - a)
Applying operations left-to-right ignoring precedence
Treating a variable as a fixed unknown rather than a placeholder
Adding unlike terms (e.g., 3x + 2 = 5x)
Combining terms across the equals sign without balancing
Distributing only to the first term: a(b + c) = ab + c
Sign error on distribution: -(a + b) = -a + b
Dividing only one side when isolating variable
Performing operations in wrong order (dividing before subtracting constant)
Moving variable term but keeping same sign
Expanding brackets before resolving the equation structure
Inverting rise/run (slope = Δx/Δy instead of Δy/Δx)
Sign error on slope with negative coordinates
Confusing m and b in y = mx + b
Treating y-intercept as a point (x, b) rather than (0, b)
Plotting (b, m) instead of (0, b) for the y-intercept
Using wrong point when writing point-slope form
Confusing one equation with two unknowns as immediately solvable
Substituting back into the same equation used for isolation
Forgetting to substitute into BOTH original equations to verify
Multiplying only one equation's coefficient when aligning terms
Adding instead of subtracting equations to eliminate (sign confusion)
Interpreting 'no solution' as 'infinitely many' and vice versa
